jp.title: ViVi国宝級イケメンランキング：SixTONES松村北斗が「ADULT部門」で首位 前人未踏の3部門殿堂入り 「僕をいいと思ってくれる人がいると思うと自信に繋がる」 2026年上半期のランキング発表
jp.description: 人気グループ「SixTONES（ストーンズ）」の松村北斗さんが、女性ファッション誌「ViVi（ヴィヴィ）」（講談社）の名物企画「ViVi国宝級イケメンランキング」の2026年上半期のランキングで、3…

jp.title: SixTONES松村北斗、「ViVi国宝級イケメンランキング」初の3部門殿堂入り達成「シンプルにうれしい」【一覧掲載】
jp.description: ニュース｜　6人組グループ・SixTONESの松村北斗が、23日発売のファッション雑誌『ViVi』9月号（講談社）で特集される人気企画「ViVi国宝級イケメンランキング」2026年上半期のADULT部門（30歳以上）で首位を獲得。主要3部門（NEXT（22歳以下）、NOW、ADULT）すべてで殿堂入りとなる同誌初のグランドスラムを達成した。　ADULT部門は、30歳以上が対象となり、過去には町田啓太、山田裕貴、坂口健太郎など、日本の名作を支える俳優たちが殿堂入りしてきた。松村は、2度目となる1位に輝いた。松村は、前例のない“国宝級グランドスラム”を達成した。

world.title: New malaria drug candidate shows promise in Phase I trial
world.description: The early-stage trial of a potential novel nonartemisinin malaria drug found it rapidly killed malaria parasites and was generally well tolerated in infected volunteers, showing it is a promising candidate for uncomplicated malaria that could help meet the need for new medicines to overcome the growing threat of parasite drug resistance.

world.title: Mapping the seafloor: How deep can we go?
world.description: Australia's ocean territory is vast and covers an area more than 1.5 times the nation's landmass. Within these waters is a diversity of resources, marine species and habitats. And they're deep. Excluding Antarctic waters, 70% of Australia's ocean territory is deeper than 1,000 meters (3,281 feet), and nearly 50% is deeper than 3,000 meters (9,843 feet).

world.title: India bets billions on breaking China's grip on smartphone manufacturing
world.description: New Delhi announced a $6.5 billion smartphone manufacturing program and a $13.3 billion semiconductor push to deepen India's electronics supply chain.

world.title: Highlights: Day 6 of Sara Duterte impeachment trial | July 15, 2026
world.description: No witnesses took the stand during the sixth day of Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial on Wednesday.

world.title: The hidden skeleton “gatekeeper” inside brain cells could help fight Alzheimer's
world.description: Researchers have discovered that a microscopic skeleton inside neurons does much more than hold cells together. It acts as a gatekeeper that controls what brain cells absorb and when they absorb it. When this protective structure weakens, neurons rapidly take in harmful proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease, suggesting that stabilizing it could become a promising new strategy for preventing brain cell damage.
